SEARCH
Package jGameLib.ecs

Class GameState

java.lang.Object
jGameLib.ecs.GameState
Direct Known Subclasses:
InstantaneousGameState
public abstract class GameState extends Object
An abstract class representing a game state in a StateMachine

    Constructs a GameState

  • Constructor Details

    • GameState

      protected GameState()
      Constructs a GameState

  • Method Details

    • addSystem

      protected void addSystem(JSystem system)
      Adds a JSystem specific to this state to be run in the game loop

    • iteratorOver

      public static Iterator<GameState> iteratorOver(GameState... states)
      A utility method to create an iterator over a list of GameStates

    • iteratorOver

      public static <T> Iterator<GameState> iteratorOver(Collection<T> list, Function<T,GameState> func)
      A utility method to convert a Collection into an iterator over GameStates using the given mapping function

    • makeWhileLoopIterator

      public static Iterator<GameState> makeWhileLoopIterator(Supplier<GameState> gameStateSupplier, Supplier<Boolean> condition)
      A utility method to make a while-loop-like iterator over GameStates
      Parameters:
      gameStateSupplier - A function with zero parameters that returns a state, to be called repeatedly in the loop
      condition - The condition of the while loop, which should return true as long as the loop should execute

    • joinIterators

      @SafeVarargs public static Iterator<GameState> joinIterators(Iterator<? extends GameState>... its)
      A utility method to join a list of Iterators over GameStates into a single iterator

    • toGameState

      public static GameState toGameState(Supplier<Iterator<GameState>> gameStateIterator)
      A utility method to convert a method that returns an Iterator over GameStates into a single state

    • groupStates

      public static GameState groupStates(GameState... states)
      A utility method to creates a single GameState out of a list of states

    • getStatesBefore

      protected Iterator<? extends GameState> getStatesBefore()
      Should probably be overridden. Returns an Iterator of GameStates that should be run before this state executes, may return an indefinite number of states. This is queried immediately after the onSchedule() method is called.

    • getStatesAfter

      protected Iterator<? extends GameState> getStatesAfter()
      Should probably be overridden. Returns an Iterator of GameStates that should be run after this state executes, may return an indefinite number of states. This is queried immediately after the onExecutionEnd() method is called.

    • onUpdate

      protected void onUpdate()
      Should probably be overridden. Called every frame before JSystems are run. Does nothing by default.

    • onSchedule

      protected void onSchedule()
      Can be overridden. Called as soon as the GameState is scheduled. Any initialization up not done in the constructor should happen here. Does nothing by default.

    • onExecutionStart

      protected void onExecutionStart()
      Can be overridden. Called before the first time JSystems and onUpdate() are run. Does nothing by default.

    • onExecutionEnd

      protected void onExecutionEnd()
      Can be overridden. Called immediately after the isFinished() returns true. Does nothing by default.

    • isFinished

      protected boolean isFinished()
      Can be overridden. Returns whether the GameState has finished executing. When this returns true, the StateMachine will not stop executing the state without running JSystems or onUpdate(). This method returns false by default, meaning that the state will never finish

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
